concerns.text: FR-010 ('System MUST apply a family-wise error correction...') is a duplicate of FR-005. FR-005 already mandates: '...AND apply a family-wise error correction (Bonferroni or Holm) to the resulting p-values before determining statistical significance.' This creates a redundant requirement with no unique scope, violating the principle of non-redundant requirements. It should be removed or merged.

FR-008 ('System MUST apply a family-wise error correction... if multiple task types are tested simultaneously') is effectively covered by the 'multiple comparisons' clause in FR-005 and the general scope of FR-010. While FR-008 adds a specific condition ('multiple task types'), the requirement to correct for multiplicity is already established in FR-005. This creates potential ambiguity on whether FR-005 applies only to noise thresholds or all multiplicity. Clarify if FR-008 is a distinct requirement or a clarification of FR-005.

FR-003 contains a complex conditional logic regarding 'validity collapse point' and exclusion of sigma values. While anchored to US-2, the phrasing 'If any sigma > 0.2 causes semantic validity failure... the system MUST record the 'validity collapse point'' introduces a specific metric ('validity collapse point') that is not explicitly defined in the User Story acceptance scenarios or the Success Criteria. Ensure this metric is either defined in the Key Entities or explicitly referenced in SC-002/SC-003 to avoid silent under-specification of the output format.
